mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index signals contemporaneous real economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment below 4.5% signals a tight labor market that raises wage-push inflation fears and Fed hawkishness, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months when UNRATE is below 4.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and a falling unemployment rate signal an overheating economy that prompts accelerated monetary tightening, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and UNRATE level does not significantly predict negative 60-day returns (t-stat > -1.5) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate raise discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity valuations and predicting negative S&P 500 20-day returns.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, months with FEDFUNDS increases fail to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding its trailing 4-quarter average indicates accelerating aggregate demand, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds its 4-quarter average, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate crossing above 1.5% marks the exit from accommodative monetary policy, triggering a broad repricing of equity risk premiums that predicts negative S&P 500 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months after FEDFUNDS first crosses above 1.5% over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,250,000 units signal weakening residential investment and slowing credit-sensitive demand, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1250 are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.
